question_title: Who will win the 2026 Masters Tournament?
summary: The 2026 Masters (April 9-12) has Scottie Scheffler as the clear betting favorite (+275 to +300) given his world #1 ranking, 4 major championships including 2 Masters wins (2022, 2024), and a strong 2026 season start. Rory McIlroy is defending champion with excellent early-season form but back-to-back Masters wins are historically rare. Jon Rahm (2023 champion) and Bryson DeChambeau are viable but play on LIV Golf with limited major prep. The "Other golfer" option captures the deep field (~90 players) including strong contenders like Fleetwood, Schauffele, Morikawa, Aberg, and Rose. Historically, someone outside the top 4 betting favorites wins the Masters ~55-65% of the time. Probabilities are derived from vig-adjusted betting odds with minor form-based adjustments.

factors.description: Scheffler is world #1, 4-time major champion, 2-time Masters winner (2022, 2024), clear betting favorite at +275 to +300. Won first 2026 start at American Express. Dominant force at Augusta with proven track record.

McIlroy is defending champion (2025), world #2, with strong early-season form (T3 Dubai, runner-up Genesis). Playing elite tee-to-green golf. However, back-to-back Masters wins are extremely rare historically (last: Tiger Woods 2001-02).

Rahm is 2023 Masters champion playing on LIV Golf. Finished 2nd at LIV Adelaide showing strong form. But LIV schedule limits Augusta-specific preparation and competitive sharpness in 72-hole stroke play formats. Odds at +1000 to +1400.
DeChambeau is a 2-time major champion on LIV Golf. T3 at LIV Adelaide. Odds at +1000 to +1200. Similar LIV prep concerns as Rahm, and has never won at Augusta.

Deep field of ~90 players includes many strong contenders: Fleetwood (+1600), Schauffele (+1600-1800), Morikawa (+2500, won Pebble Beach in 2026), Aberg (+2600), Rose (+2500-3000, won Farmers), Reed (+2800, dominating DP World Tour), Gotterup (2-time 2026 winner). Historically 55-65% of Masters are won by someone outside top 4 favorites.
Scheffler's documented Thursday slow starts in 2026 (5-over par on opening days in last 3 events) could be an exploitable vulnerability at Augusta, slightly boosting field chances.

reasoning:
## Forecast: 2026 Masters Tournament Winner

| Option | Probability |
|--------|------------|
| **Scottie Scheffler** | **19%** |
| **Rory McIlroy** | **10%** |
| **Jon Rahm** | **6%** |
| **Bryson DeChambeau** | **6%** |
| **Other golfer** | **59%** |

**Key reasoning:**

- **Scheffler (19%)** — World #1, 4-time major champion with 2 Masters wins (2022, 2024). Clear betting favorite at +275-300. Won his first 2026 start, though Thursday slow starts are a noted concern.

- **McIlroy (10%)** — Defending champion with elite early-season form (runner-up at Genesis). But back-to-back Masters wins are historically rare (last: Tiger 2001-02).

- **Rahm & DeChambeau (6% each)** — Both past major champions playing on LIV Golf with strong recent form (2nd and T3 at LIV Adelaide). LIV schedule limits major prep, and odds reflect their lower-tier contender status at +1000-1400.

- **Other golfer (59%)** — The deep field includes Fleetwood, Schauffele, Morikawa (won Pebble Beach), Aberg, Rose (won Farmers at 45), Reed (dominating DP World Tour), and Gotterup (2-time 2026 winner). Historically, ~55-65% of Masters are won by someone outside the top 4 favorites.

resolution_criteria: This question resolves to the option corresponding to the official tournament winner listed for the 2026 Masters on Masters.com. If the event is not completed and no winner is listed before May 1, 2026, this question resolves to “No champion declared.” The primary source is Masters.com past winners/results: https://www.masters.com/en_US/tournament/past_winners.html
fine_print: If the winner is not one of the named golfer options, resolve to “Other golfer.” If the primary source is unavailable, use the PGA TOUR tournament results page as a backup: https://www.pgatour.com/tournaments/2026/masters-tournament/R2026014/past-results The question may also resolve based on [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions)
background_info:
This forecasts the champion golfer of the 2026 Masters, one of golf’s four major championships.
